headline: Head-high-plus S swell but cross-on wind all day keeps it rough.
today: Plenty of size, pushing 5ft-plus at times with a good angle, so there's real energy. Problem is the SW/SSW cross-on blows all day, so it'll be bumpy and disorganized. Surfable on a shortboard for the committed, but no glass to be found.

daysToWatch.note: Flag this one hard: 3.6-4.7ft at a good angle with N offshore morning going near-glassy light NW in the afternoon. Solid, clean, worth rearranging plans. Shortboard day.
Still fun at 2.2-3.2ft, prime SSE angle with N offshore dawn. Get the early window before the SE cross-on builds through the day.
Prime angle but small and windy — 15-17mph NE/E cross running all day. Choppy and marginal, probably a skip unless you like sideshore chop.
Small swell and wind ramping to 19-23mph cross-on by afternoon — blown out later in the day.
